Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265. This was little more than some back-and-forth over a couple of systems, but the length of time it occurred for earned itself it’s own rating as a war.image493×591 25.1 KB12433 Won with +3 planets12430 v 12433 II - Full WarA full 55 ticks after the last attack during the skirmish, 12430 started up hostilities vs 12433 again, this time following up the initial attacks with a war declaration. The war lasted until T396, with 12433 eventually gaining the advantageimage493×583 28.2 KB12433 Won with +21 planets12431 vs 12429 - Big vs Small with DeletionsBased on planet change data, from T385 until T404 12431 waged war against 12429. The end of this war, and the actual planets won and lost is hard to determine as most of 12429 deleted, and this deletion caused a mini-expo phase and the end of the war.image493×589 24.7 KB12431 Won with +52 planets12431 v 12432 - Mini-expo warDue to the deletion of 12429 and the mass amounts of empty planets, there was a scramble by all fams to claim the explorations. 12431 then also cleared 12432 up after this mini-expo phase. Planet gains are approximated based on planet changes. Other fams were also hitting both fams to clear some core as well. However, 12431 had a more concerted and longer effort to hit 12432, lasting from T400 - T417.image495×590 25.2 KB12431 Won with +22 planets12434 v 12431 - The last warRight at the end of the mid-round, there was one last big war as 12434 hit 12431 with everything they had in an attempt to make gains before the end of round. Both fams jumped big, with 12434 jumping 6mill NW, or 140% of their pre-war NW. The initial gains were to 12434, but the greater economy of 12431 was not disrupted enough, and soon after the initial salvo they jumped nearly 4mill (+40% nw). However, 12431 were able to hold their NW better, and 12434 started to lose more. Eventually the war ended, and 12431 were the winnersimage494×587 27.7 KB12431 Won with +23 planetsMid-Round Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#1243145612,127,61398,490,3932#124333464,050,1833,282,1103#124343239,156,09237,276,1894#124302393,068,3502,143,7905#124321854,734,12268,829,6676#1242956350,219545,440End of RoundRound OverviewOverall the round was won by 12431 with 477 planets and 21,898,681 NW. Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265. Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265. This was little more than some back-and-forth over a couple of systems, but the length of time it occurred for earned itself it’s own rating as a war.image493×591 25.1 KB12433 Won with +3 planets12430 v 12433 II - Full WarA full 55 ticks after the last attack during the skirmish, 12430 started up hostilities vs 12433 again, this time following up the initial attacks with a war declaration. The war lasted until T396, with 12433 eventually gaining the advantageimage493×583 28.2 KB12433 Won with +21 planets12431 vs 12429 - Big vs Small with DeletionsBased on planet change data, from T385 until T404 12431 waged war against 12429. The end of this war, and the actual planets won and lost is hard to determine as most of 12429 deleted, and this deletion caused a mini-expo phase and the end of the war.image493×589 24.7 KB12431 Won with +52 planets12431 v 12432 - Mini-expo warDue to the deletion of 12429 and the mass amounts of empty planets, there was a scramble by all fams to claim the explorations. 12431 then also cleared 12432 up after this mini-expo phase. Planet gains are approximated based on planet changes. Other fams were also hitting both fams to clear some core as well. However, 12431 had a more concerted and longer effort to hit 12432, lasting from T400 - T417.image495×590 25.2 KB12431 Won with +22 planets12434 v 12431 - The last warRight at the end of the mid-round, there was one last big war as 12434 hit 12431 with everything they had in an attempt to make gains before the end of round. Both fams jumped big, with 12434 jumping 6mill NW, or 140% of their pre-war NW. The initial gains were to 12434, but the greater economy of 12431 was not disrupted enough, and soon after the initial salvo they jumped nearly 4mill (+40% nw). However, 12431 were able to hold their NW better, and 12434 started to lose more. Eventually the war ended, and 12431 were the winnersimage494×587 27.7 KB12431 Won with +23 planetsMid-Round Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#1243145612,127,61398,490,3932#124333464,050,1833,282,1103#124343239,156,09237,276,1894#124302393,068,3502,143,7905#124321854,734,12268,829,6676#1242956350,219545,440End of RoundRound OverviewOverall the round was won by 12431 with 477 planets and 21,898,681 NW. Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265. This was little more than some back-and-forth over a couple of systems, but the length of time it occurred for earned itself it’s own rating as a war.image493×591 25.1 KB12433 Won with +3 planets12430 v 12433 II - Full WarA full 55 ticks after the last attack during the skirmish, 12430 started up hostilities vs 12433 again, this time following up the initial attacks with a war declaration. The war lasted until T396, with 12433 eventually gaining the advantageimage493×583 28.2 KB12433 Won with +21 planets12431 vs 12429 - Big vs Small with DeletionsBased on planet change data, from T385 until T404 12431 waged war against 12429. The end of this war, and the actual planets won and lost is hard to determine as most of 12429 deleted, and this deletion caused a mini-expo phase and the end of the war.image493×589 24.7 KB12431 Won with +52 planets12431 v 12432 - Mini-expo warDue to the deletion of 12429 and the mass amounts of empty planets, there was a scramble by all fams to claim the explorations. 12431 then also cleared 12432 up after this mini-expo phase. Planet gains are approximated based on planet changes. Other fams were also hitting both fams to clear some core as well. However, 12431 had a more concerted and longer effort to hit 12432, lasting from T400 - T417.image495×590 25.2 KB12431 Won with +22 planets12434 v 12431 - The last warRight at the end of the mid-round, there was one last big war as 12434 hit 12431 with everything they had in an attempt to make gains before the end of round. Both fams jumped big, with 12434 jumping 6mill NW, or 140% of their pre-war NW. The initial gains were to 12434, but the greater economy of 12431 was not disrupted enough, and soon after the initial salvo they jumped nearly 4mill (+40% nw). However, 12431 were able to hold their NW better, and 12434 started to lose more. Eventually the war ended, and 12431 were the winnersimage494×587 27.7 KB12431 Won with +23 planetsMid-Round Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#1243145612,127,61398,490,3932#124333464,050,1833,282,1103#124343239,156,09237,276,1894#124302393,068,3502,143,7905#124321854,734,12268,829,6676#1242956350,219545,440End of RoundRound OverviewOverall the round was won by 12431 with 477 planets and 21,898,681 NW. Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265. This was little more than some back-and-forth over a couple of systems, but the length of time it occurred for earned itself it’s own rating as a war.image493×591 25.1 KB12433 Won with +3 planets12430 v 12433 II - Full WarA full 55 ticks after the last attack during the skirmish, 12430 started up hostilities vs 12433 again, this time following up the initial attacks with a war declaration. The war lasted until T396, with 12433 eventually gaining the advantageimage493×583 28.2 KB12433 Won with +21 planets12431 vs 12429 - Big vs Small with DeletionsBased on planet change data, from T385 until T404 12431 waged war against 12429. The end of this war, and the actual planets won and lost is hard to determine as most of 12429 deleted, and this deletion caused a mini-expo phase and the end of the war.image493×589 24.7 KB12431 Won with +52 planets12431 v 12432 - Mini-expo warDue to the deletion of 12429 and the mass amounts of empty planets, there was a scramble by all fams to claim the explorations. 12431 then also cleared 12432 up after this mini-expo phase. Planet gains are approximated based on planet changes. Other fams were also hitting both fams to clear some core as well. However, 12431 had a more concerted and longer effort to hit 12432, lasting from T400 - T417.image495×590 25.2 KB12431 Won with +22 planets12434 v 12431 - The last warRight at the end of the mid-round, there was one last big war as 12434 hit 12431 with everything they had in an attempt to make gains before the end of round. Both fams jumped big, with 12434 jumping 6mill NW, or 140% of their pre-war NW. The initial gains were to 12434, but the greater economy of 12431 was not disrupted enough, and soon after the initial salvo they jumped nearly 4mill (+40% nw). However, 12431 were able to hold their NW better, and 12434 started to lose more. Eventually the war ended, and 12431 were the winnersimage494×587 27.7 KB12431 Won with +23 planetsMid-Round Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#1243145612,127,61398,490,3932#124333464,050,1833,282,1103#124343239,156,09237,276,1894#124302393,068,3502,143,7905#124321854,734,12268,829,6676#1242956350,219545,440End of RoundRound OverviewOverall the round was won by 12431 with 477 planets and 21,898,681 NW. Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265. This was little more than some back-and-forth over a couple of systems, but the length of time it occurred for earned itself it’s own rating as a war.image493×591 25.1 KB12433 Won with +3 planets12430 v 12433 II - Full WarA full 55 ticks after the last attack during the skirmish, 12430 started up hostilities vs 12433 again, this time following up the initial attacks with a war declaration. The war lasted until T396, with 12433 eventually gaining the advantageimage493×583 28.2 KB12433 Won with +21 planets12431 vs 12429 - Big vs Small with DeletionsBased on planet change data, from T385 until T404 12431 waged war against 12429. The end of this war, and the actual planets won and lost is hard to determine as most of 12429 deleted, and this deletion caused a mini-expo phase and the end of the war.image493×589 24.7 KB12431 Won with +52 planets12431 v 12432 - Mini-expo warDue to the deletion of 12429 and the mass amounts of empty planets, there was a scramble by all fams to claim the explorations. 12431 then also cleared 12432 up after this mini-expo phase. Planet gains are approximated based on planet changes. Other fams were also hitting both fams to clear some core as well. However, 12431 had a more concerted and longer effort to hit 12432, lasting from T400 - T417.image495×590 25.2 KB12431 Won with +22 planets12434 v 12431 - The last warRight at the end of the mid-round, there was one last big war as 12434 hit 12431 with everything they had in an attempt to make gains before the end of round. Both fams jumped big, with 12434 jumping 6mill NW, or 140% of their pre-war NW. The initial gains were to 12434, but the greater economy of 12431 was not disrupted enough, and soon after the initial salvo they jumped nearly 4mill (+40% nw). However, 12431 were able to hold their NW better, and 12434 started to lose more. Eventually the war ended, and 12431 were the winnersimage494×587 27.7 KB12431 Won with +23 planetsMid-Round Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#1243145612,127,61398,490,3932#124333464,050,1833,282,1103#124343239,156,09237,276,1894#124302393,068,3502,143,7905#124321854,734,12268,829,6676#1242956350,219545,440End of RoundRound OverviewOverall the round was won by 12431 with 477 planets and 21,898,681 NW. Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Notes on Elo and War ScoreNotes on War ScoreWar score is based on the calculations for victory in Clan Wars. Over course of last few rounds it has shown as a good indicator for war winner prediction, as well as indicator for most war like family. War score is shown here for information purposes. All war summaries use potential war score (war score calculated at start of war) to determine odds of declaring fam winning war.Declaring Fam Win % = 1- [Fam(Decl) Potential score / Sum of both potential score]Notes on Elo RatingElo rating is a system (normally used for games involving 2 players) that attempts to rate a players expected performance based on past performances. I have adapted it for use in IC in 3 ways. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. First is to give a running score based on NW ranking, second the same for Size ranking, and lastly as a rating on war performance.Ratings are biased towards war performance.The Elo rating should show expected outcomes in future ticks, i.e. high Elo should indicate that a fam will be higher ranked in Size and NW rankings, and more likely to win any war when compared to a lower Elo rated fam. Is a good indicator of war performance. Elo ratings could be used to later determine player ratings.This was calculated using the standard Elo calculation, modified slightlyElo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(size) * (S(size) - E) + k(NW) * (S(NW) - E) + dElo(War)Where:Elo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
   Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
   k(Size)    =   Elo coefficient for Size ranking (currently set to `5`)
   k(NW)      =   Elo coefficient for NW ranking (currently set to `5)
   S(Size)    =   Modified Size Rank result this tick
   S(NW)      =   Modified NW Rank result this tick
   E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingThe modified ranks are calculated by dividing the actual rank by the number of families in the galaxy less 1S = Rank / [Num(fam) - 1]Eis calculated based off the last ticks Elo for the fam, and the average Elo for all fams in the galaxyE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(MeanElo / 400)The dElo(War) is the change (delta) in Elo ratings from wars. It is calculated as follows (Standard Elo calculation)Elo(n) = Elo(n-1) + k(war) * (S - E)WhereElo(n)     =   Elo rating this tick
    Elo(n-1)   =   Elo rating last tick
    k(war)     =   Elo coefficient for wars (currently set to 50)
    S          =   Result of war (Win S = 1, Loss S = 0)
    E          =   Expected result based on Elo ratingEis calculated similar to NW and Size ranking Elo, but uses the Elo of the 2nd fam in the warE = R1 / (R1 + R2 )WhereR1 = 10^(Elo(Fam1 n-1) / 400)
R2 = 10^(Elo(Fam2 n-1) / 400)Elo(n) is worked out for each fam (R1 and R2 are swapped for each fam)Calculation for War ScorePotential War Score = modifier * Size Score + NW(modified) ScoreWhereModifier = [1- % difference in planets] if difference is more than 15%
Size Score = 10 * [ Number of Planets of fam at start of war / Number of planets of other fam at start of war]
NW(modified) Score = 10 * [NW of fam at start of war less NW due to planets / NW of other fam at start of war less NW due to planets]After war ends, the final score is determined based on who won the most planets, and how many planets they wonFinal Score = Potential Score  * Winning Modifier * Farming ModifierWhereWinning Modifier = 100% if gain more planets (Win) or 25% if lose more planets (Lose)
Farming Modifier = 100% if less than 15% of planets lost, 75% of 15-25% of planets lost, 50% if 25-35% of planets lost, 25% if 35-50% of planets lost, 0% if more than 50% of planets lost

Exploration PhasePhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT1T25037.1% of the RoundOverviewThe exploration phase lasted 250 ticks, when the last two planets were explored by Pjeewpjeew infam #12431This meant that the overall rate of exploration for the round was 6.81 planets per tick, or 163.44 planets explored per day. There were 28 empires during exploration phase (or at least the vast majority), so the total number of exploration ships available were 2,333 during that time, or 224 ships per day. Thus the exploration efficiency was 74.3%. In comparison, the last two Triangulum rounds that were recorded (Tri 27 and 28) had exploration rates of 5.88 and 6.3 expos per tick respectively. So last round had a much more efficient exploration rate nearly 2 years on.Family ExplorationAs expected, different families explored at different rates. Also, unsurprisingly, the families that explored the fastest tended to end near the top of the rankings.Fam 12431explored at 1.5 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 89.8% efficiency)Fam 12433explored at 1.33 planets per tick (out of max 1.67 planets per tick | 79.6% efficiency)Fam 12434explored at 1.19 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 89.5% efficiency)Fam 12430explored at 1.14 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 85.7% efficiency)Fam 12432explored at 0.84 planets per tick (out of max 1.00 planets per tick | 84.0% efficiency )Fam 12429explored at 0.81 planets per tick (out of max 1.33 planets per tick | 60.9% efficiency)Most of the above is impacted by player numbers per family and activity level. Fam 12429 especially was hit hard with several people joining, exploring 1 round, then deleting along with their planets. A more realistic efficiency is 81.0% based on the count of players that stayed in the fam over the course of the whole exploration phase.Exploration Phase WarsAdded to the complexity of the exploration rates, is the fact there were at least 2 expo wars (maybe more but exploration can cover most). These wars were hardly more than core-establisment skirmishes, but two at least lasted for long enough and with enough back and forth trading to count as full wars12431 v 12433 - Early Expo War12431 won a war vs 12433 as both stabilized their core border. This war lasted from T26 to T73 when the borders stabilized. No official NAP was signed at that point, but captures stopped after that and both sides had a stable core.image497×588 26.3 KB12431 won with +5 planets (approximate based on fam news reports)12431 v 12429 - Late Expo-WarA late exploration phase war sprung up as 12431 hit 12429 for approximately 17 planets from T211 to T245. Most of the damage was done from T218 - T225 with 12429 losing 13 planets during this time. Once again some of the impacts are hard to gauge as exploration was still ongoing.image496×586 24.8 KB12431 won with +17 planets (approximate based on total planets lost by 12429End of Exploration Phase RankingsRankFamilyPlanetsNetworthPopulation1#12431386144693848062692#12433305102527322726313#124342821525778141032554#12430261131764221215235#12429187103216741198826#12432185113037910047395Mid-Round Skirmishes and GrowthPhase Start TickPhase End tickPercent of RoundT250T57548.2% of the RoundOverviewMost of the rest of the round was dominated by long periods of economic growth and a few major conflicts. Overall the NW of the galaxy grew by approximately 60,000 nw/tick.There were 3 main conflicts, 12430 & 12433, then 12433 & 12429 (which triggered a lot of 12429 deletions) and then 12434 & 12431.Wars12430 vs 12433 I - First Skirmish12430 first tried to take some planets off 12433 from T220 till T265.
